articles.title: Novelis restarts Oswego hot mill after nine months of fire-related shutdown
articles.summary: Novelis restarted its hot mill in Oswego, New York on 10 June 2026, ending a nine-month outage caused by two fires in September and November 2025. The facility supplies over a dozen automakers including Ford, GM, and Stellantis, and is ramping up below full capacity as production resumes.

articles.title: Hindalco shares rise 4% on strong Novelis Q4; Oswego restart lifts outlook
articles.summary: Hindalco shares rose 4% on 10 June as markets reacted to the Oswego hot mill restart alongside Novelis FY26 full-year results, which showed adjusted EBITDA of $1.6 billion (down 9% year-on-year) and net sales of $18.4 billion (up 7% year-on-year).

articles.title: Germany's aluminium recycling output drops 3% year-on-year; recovery looks lean in 2026
articles.summary: Germany's recycled aluminium production fell 3% year-on-year to 684,564 tonnes in Q1 2026, according to Aluminium Deutschland data. High energy costs, weak demand, and scrap shortages are compounding sector pressure, with 57% of companies not expecting conditions to improve by year-end and 66% rating order situations as poor or very poor.
articles.novelis_angle: Scrap tightness in Germany, where Novelis operates Nachterstedt and other FRP plants, raises competition for post-consumer aluminium and risks increasing closed-loop input costs against the company's 75% recycled-content and 3×30 targets by 2030.

articles.title: Geopolitical disturbances weaken marginally, accelerating destocking supports aluminium prices to fluctuate upward
articles.summary: LME aluminium traded at USD 3,532.5/t on 10 June, testing 60-day moving average support at USD 3,526.37, while SHFE aluminium settled at RMB 23,900/t with trading volume at 81,600 lots, signalling weak market sentiment. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East eased marginally, with accelerating downstream destocking expected to provide near-term upward price support.
